title: Support 'this' keyword inside inline randomize() with {} constraint blocks (#7102)
body:
## Summary
Support using `this.member` inside inline `randomize() with {}` constraint blocks when called from outside a class (e.g., module-level code). Per IEEE 1800-2023 18.7, `this` in an inline constraint should refer to the object being randomized.

## Issue
Fixes #7102 

## Changes
- `src/V3LinkDot.cpp`: In `LinkDotResolveVisitor::visit(AstDot*)`, when `this` is the LHS and `getThisClassSymp()` returns nullptr, fall back to `m_randSymp` (which holds the randomized object's class symbol table, set by the `AstMethodCall` visitor for `randomize()`).

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_randomize_this_inline.{v,py}` — tests `this.member` in inline constraints from module-level code, including multiple references and mixed qualified/unqualified access.

---
Written by Claude Code and PlanV GmbH.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
